glass_card.name: Glass Card
glass_card.description: Frosted glass card with depth and subtle glow. Use for primary content cards, stat cards, and elevated surfaces.
glass_card.when_to_use: Any card that needs to feel elevated above the background. Dashboard stat cards, feature cards, pricing cards.
glass_card.light_variant: bg-white/70 backdrop-blur-xl border border-black/[0.06] rounded-2xl shadow-lg shadow-black/5
glass_card.hover: hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-lg hover:shadow-primary/10 hover:border-primary/20 transition-all duration-300

gradient_accent.name: Gradient Accent
gradient_accent.description: Gradient backgrounds for CTAs, badges, and accent elements. Never use raw solid colors for primary buttons — gradients add depth.
gradient_accent.when_to_use: Primary CTA buttons, active navigation indicators, brand elements, badges.
gradient_accent.tailwind_button: bg-gradient-to-br from-primary to-primary/80 shadow-lg shadow-primary/25 border-0
gradient_accent.hover: hover:brightness-110 hover:shadow-xl hover:shadow-primary/30 active:brightness-95 transition-all duration-200

depth_layers.name: Depth & Layering System
depth_layers.description: A 4-level depth system using background colors, borders, and shadows. This is what separates flat vibecoded UIs from designed ones.

raised.description: Sidebar, header backgrounds. One level up.

overlay.description: Tooltips, popovers, command palettes. Highest level.

depth_layers.rule: Every UI element must belong to exactly one depth level. No two adjacent elements should be on the same level — if they look flat, one needs to go up or down.

text_hierarchy.name: Text Hierarchy System
text_hierarchy.description: A 4-tier text color system. This is the #1 thing that makes UI look designed vs vibecoded.

secondary.use: Body text, table cell content, descriptions

text_hierarchy.rule: Every text element must use exactly one tier. If you find yourself using the same tier for a label and its value, the hierarchy is wrong — the value should be one tier higher.

icon_treatment.name: Icon Treatment
icon_treatment.description: How to make icons look professional instead of amateur.
rules: NEVER use emoji in professional UI. Use Lucide, Heroicons, or Phosphor.
Icons in navigation: 16-18px, stroke-width 1.5-2, match text color tier
Icons in stat cards: wrap in a 40px rounded-xl container with tinted background (primary/10)
Icons in buttons: 16px, same color as button text, placed before the label
Decorative icons: can be larger (24-32px) but lower opacity
icon_treatment.stat_card_icon: w-10 h-10 rounded-xl flex items-center justify-center bg-primary/10

motion_system.name: Motion & Animation
motion_system.description: Entrance animations and micro-interactions that make UI feel alive.

entrance.stagger_cards: Stagger each card by 80-120ms. Use translateY(16px) → 0 + opacity 0 → 1. Easing: cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94)
entrance.fade_section: Sections fade in on scroll with IntersectionObserver or Framer Motion whileInView
entrance.table_rows: Rows stagger in with 40-60ms delay each, opacity only

micro.nav_active: Background color transition 200ms with subtle left-border or dot indicator
micro.row_hover: Background color shift + text color accent on the primary column

framer_motion_snippet.stagger_container: { initial: 'hidden', animate: 'show', variants: { show: { transition: { staggerChildren: 0.1 } } } }
framer_motion_snippet.stagger_item: { variants: { hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 16 }, show: { opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: { duration: 0.5, ease: [0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94] } } } }

spacing_rhythm.name: Spacing & Rhythm
spacing_rhythm.description: Intentional spacing creates visual rhythm. Equal spacing everywhere = boring.
rules: Section gaps: 24-32px (gap-6 to gap-8). NOT equal to card internal padding.
Card internal padding: 20-24px (p-5 to p-6).
Between label and value: 4-8px (space-y-1 to space-y-2).
Between icon container and text: 12-16px (gap-3 to gap-4).
Header to content: 24px. Content sections: 24px. Footer: 12-16px.
Sidebar width: 220-260px. NOT 300px (wastes space).
Table cell padding: px-6 py-3.5 for comfortable density, px-4 py-2.5 for compact.
anti_patterns: Card internal spacing matches section spacing

surface_texture.name: Surface Texture
surface_texture.description: Subtle visual texture prevents the 'flat digital void' look.

techniques.noise_overlay: Subtle SVG noise at 1-2% opacity over the base background. Adds analog warmth.
techniques.gradient_mesh: Very subtle radial gradients (opacity 5-10%) behind key content areas to create focal points.
techniques.border_glow: Cards with box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.06) instead of solid borders. Creates glass-like edge.
techniques.inner_glow: Radial gradient at 30% 30% position inside containers for a subtle light source effect.

surface_texture.gradient_mesh_css: background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 20% 50%, rgba(var(--accent-rgb), 0.08), transparent 60%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 80% 20%, rgba(var(--accent-rgb), 0.05), transparent 50%);

data_visualization.name: Chart & Data Polish
data_visualization.description: How to make charts look designed instead of default Recharts output.
rules: Remove axis lines (axisLine={false}), keep only tick labels
Use dashed grid lines at very low opacity (stroke rgba(255,255,255,0.04))
Area charts: gradient fill from color/25 to transparent, stroke-width 2-2.5
Donut charts: add center label with total value
Custom tooltip: glass card style, small text (text-xs), compact padding
Use brand colors for data series, not random Recharts defaults
Legend: inline in the card header, not below the chart
data_visualization.area_gradient: linearGradient from stopColor={color} stopOpacity={0.25} to stopOpacity={0}
data_visualization.tooltip_style: bg-surface/95 backdrop-blur-xl border border-white/10 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-xs shadow-xl

table_polish.name: Table Design
rules: Header: uppercase tracking-wider text-[11px] font-semibold in muted color
Row hover: subtle background shift + accent color on primary column text
Status indicators: small dot (w-1.5 h-1.5) + text, NOT full badges for inline status
Amounts: tabular-nums font-feature, green for positive, red for negative
Monospace for IDs and codes: font-mono text-xs in muted color
Pagination: compact, show count ('1-10 of 247'), chevron icons not text buttons
Border: only top border on rows (border-t), not full grid

sidebar_design.name: Sidebar Navigation
sidebar_design.description: Professional sidebar that doesn't look like every template.
rules: Width: 220-260px, never 300+
Active item: gradient background (primary/20 to primary/8) with inset border, NOT solid bg-primary
Active indicator: small dot or left border accent, not just background color
Section labels: 10-11px uppercase tracking-widest in muted color
Icons: 16px stroke-[1.8], slightly bolder for active item
Spacing: py-2.5 per item, space-y-0.5 between items
Bottom section: user avatar + name, separated by border-t
Optional: upgrade CTA card with gradient background and subtle glow
